req.title: System-Admin creates document
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to create a document through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers document creation via a UI form under an authenticated system-admin role. Tests are designed around: (1) a happy-path Equivalence Partition where all mandatory fields are supplied and the document is persisted with an audit trail; (2) a Negative Path covering unauthenticated access (401); (3) a Negative Path / Decision Table covering a forbidden role (403); (4) a Boundary Value Analysis case exercising a mandatory text field at exactly its minimum length (1 char) and at zero length to probe the 400 path; (5) a State Transition Coverage case verifying the UI transitions from 'draft' form state through 'submitted' to 'created' and then confirms the audit record via API. Together these cases satisfy EP, BVA, STC, DT, and NEG heuristics and cover all stated error pathways (400, 401, 403) plus the success path (200/201).

tests.name: test_admin_creates_document_all_mandatory_fields
actions: Navigate to /documents
Fill all mandatory fields with valid representative values
Submit the form
Capture the response / resulting entity ID
Query audit log API for the created document ID
expected: Response body contains a non-empty entity ID
Audit log contains exactly one record for the new entity ID with action=CREATE
trace: R-WEB-029: audit record written on success
R-WEB-029: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_admin_creates_document_all_mandatory_fields(
    admin_browser_page,   # <fixture> authenticated Playwright page as admin
    audit_api_client,     # <fixture> requests.Session pointed at audit API
    valid_document_payload,  # <fixture> dict with all mandatory fields populated
    cleanup_document,     # <fixture> finalizer that deletes doc by id
):
    page = admin_browser_page
    page.goto("/documents")
    page.get_by_role("button", name="New Document").click()

    fill_document_form(page, valid_document_payload)  # <helper_fn>
    page.get_by_role("button", name="Submit").click()

    success_banner = page.get_by_test_id("doc-create-success")
    expect(success_banner).to_be_visible(timeout=5000)

    entity_id = success_banner.get_attribute("data-entity-id")
    assert entity_id, "Entity ID must be present in success banner"
    cleanup_document(entity_id)

    audit_resp = audit_api_client.get(f"/audit?entity_id={entity_id}&action=CREATE")
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    records = audit_resp.json().get("records", [])
    assert len(records) >= 1, "Audit record must exist for CREATE action"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_create_document
actions: Navigate directly to /documents without logging in
Attempt to POST document creation payload via unauthenticated API call
expected: UI redirects to login page OR displays 401/unauthorised message
Direct API POST to /documents returns HTTP 401
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_create_document(
    anon_browser_page,       # <fixture> Playwright page with NO auth cookies
    base_api_url,            # <fixture> str, e.g. "https://hr-app.local"
    valid_document_payload,  # <fixture> dict with all mandatory fields
):
    page = anon_browser_page
    page.goto("/documents")

    # UI should redirect or show unauthorised state
    assert (
        "/login" in page.url or
        page.get_by_text("Unauthorized").is_visible()
    ), "Unauthenticated user must not reach /documents"

    # Verify API also rejects without credentials
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_api_url}/documents",
        json=valid_document_payload,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401, (
        f"Expected 401 for unauthenticated POST, got {resp.status_code}"
    )

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_create_document
actions: Navigate to /documents as viewer-role user
Attempt to POST document creation via API with viewer credentials
expected: UI does not show 'New Document' button OR shows forbidden message
trace: R-WEB-029: error pathway - 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_forbidden_role_cannot_create_document(
    viewer_browser_page,     # <fixture> authenticated Playwright page as viewer
    viewer_api_session,      # <fixture> requests.Session with viewer auth token
    base_api_url,            # <fixture> str
    valid_document_payload,  # <fixture> dict with all mandatory fields
):
    page = viewer_browser_page
    page.goto("/documents")

    new_doc_btn = page.get_by_role("button", name="New Document")
    assert not new_doc_btn.is_visible(), (
        "'New Document' button must not be visible for viewer role"
    )

    resp = viewer_api_session.post(
        f"{base_api_url}/documents",
        json=valid_document_payload,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403, (
        f"Expected 403 for forbidden role POST, got {resp.status_code}"
    )

tests.name: test_document_title_at_minimum_boundary_length
preconditions: Minimum allowed title length is 1 character
actions: Navigate to /documents
Open New Document form
Set title field to exactly 1 character (min boundary)
Fill all other mandatory fields with valid values
Submit the form
Repeat with title field set to 0 characters (below min boundary)
expected: Title with 1 character: form submits successfully, response 200 or 201, entity created
Title with 0 characters: form shows inline validation error, response 400 from API, no entity created
trace: R-WEB-029: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-029: error pathway - 400 on validation error
R-WEB-029: positive outcome when boundary is exactly met
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

@pytest.mark.parametrize("title,expect_success", [
    ("A", True),   # min boundary: 1 char
    ("",  False),  # below min boundary: 0 chars
])
def test_document_title_at_minimum_boundary_length(
    title, expect_success,
    admin_browser_page,      # <fixture>
    base_api_url,            # <fixture>
    valid_document_payload,  # <fixture>
    cleanup_document,        # <fixture>
):
    page = admin_browser_page
    payload = {**valid_document_payload, "title": title}

    page.goto("/documents")
    page.get_by_role("button", name="New Document").click()
    fill_document_form(page, payload)  # <helper_fn>
    page.get_by_role("button", name="Submit").click()

    if expect_success:
        banner = page.get_by_test_id("doc-create-success")
        expect(banner).to_be_visible(timeout=5000)
        entity_id = banner.get_attribute("data-entity-id")
        assert entity_id
        cleanup_document(entity_id)
    else:
        err = page.get_by_test_id("field-error-title")
        expect(err).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        assert page.url.endswith("/documents/new") or "/documents" in page.url

tests.name: test_document_creation_state_transitions
actions: Navigate to /documents — verify form is in INITIAL state
Begin filling mandatory fields — verify form transitions to IN_PROGRESS state (Save Draft enabled)
Submit completed form — verify form transitions to SUBMITTED state (spinner visible)
Await confirmation — verify UI transitions to CREATED state (success banner visible)
Navigate to document detail page — verify document status is 'active'
Query audit API — verify state change event recorded
expected: UI state sequence: INITIAL -> IN_PROGRESS -> SUBMITTED -> CREATED
Audit log records CREATE event with actor=system-admin
trace: R-WEB-029: triggers - system-admin creates a document
R-WEB-029: audit record written on success
R-WEB-029: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_document_creation_state_transitions(
    admin_browser_page,      # <fixture>
    audit_api_client,        # <fixture>
    valid_document_payload,  # <fixture>
    cleanup_document,        # <fixture>
    admin_username,          # <fixture> str
):
    page = admin_browser_page
    page.goto("/documents")
    page.get_by_role("button", name="New Document").click()

    # INITIAL: form present, submit disabled
    submit_btn = page.get_by_role("button", name="Submit")
    expect(submit_btn).to_be_disabled()

    # IN_PROGRESS: filling fields enables save-draft
    fill_document_form(page, valid_document_payload)  # <helper_fn>
    expect(page.get_by_role("button", name="Save Draft")).to_be_enabled()

    # SUBMITTED: spinner appears on submit
    submit_btn.click()
    expect(page.get_by_test_id("submit-spinner")).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)

    # CREATED: success banner
    banner = page.get_by_test_id("doc-create-success")
    expect(banner).to_be_visible(timeout=8000)
    entity_id = banner.get_attribute("data-entity-id")
    assert entity_id
    cleanup_document(entity_id)

    # Verify document status on detail page
    page.goto(f"/documents/{entity_id}")
    expect(page.get_by_test_id("doc-status")).to_have_text("active")

    # Verify audit record includes actor
    records = audit_api_client.get(
        f"/audit?entity_id={entity_id}&action=CREATE"
    ).json().get("records", [])
    assert any(r.get("actor") == admin_username for r in records)
